latest.title: Faron Pharmaceuticals Ltd: Faron appoints new CTO to strengthen late-stage development activities
latest.content:
Today 07:00
Faron Pharmaceuticals Ltd | Company announcement | March 26, 2026 at 09:00:00 EET
TURKU, FINLAND â€" Faron Pharmaceuticals Ltd. (AIM: FARN, First North: FARON), a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company, announces the appointment of Mr. Heikki Jouttij¤rvi as the Companyâ€™s Chief Technical Officer (CTO).
Heikki Jouttij¤rvi brings decades of experience in biopharma manufacturing, supply chain management and commercial operations. Most recently, he has been the SVP of Commercial Business Operations at Adverum Biotechnologies, Inc that was acquired by Eli Lilly. Prior to that Mr. Jouttij¤rvi has held various global roles at Menlo Therapeutics, Inc and Santen Pharmaceutical Company, Ltd. He holds a M.Sc degree in Pharmacy from the University of Helsinki.
â€œWe are extremely happy to welcome Heikki as a part of the Faron team. His wealth of experience with commercial operations, manufacturing and supply chain management with late-stage and approved drugs is incredibly valuable for us as we progress with bexmarilimab to the announced Phase IIb trial in HR MDSâ€, comments Dr. Juho Jalkanen, CEO of Faron.â€œI am honored to join the Faron team at this exciting point in time in the development of bexmarilimab. I have been amazed by the resilience and excellent hard work done with bexmarilimab to date and I am humbled to be able to join the program at this stage. We look forward to advance the program to the benefit patients, whom deserve our ultimate commitmentâ€, says Mr. Jouttij¤rvi, newly appointed CTO of Faron Pharmaceuticals.
For more information, please contact:
IR Partners, Finland(Media)Kare Laukkanen
+358 50 553 9535 / +44 7 469 766 223
kare.laukkanen@irpartners.fi
FINN Partners, US(Media) Alyssa Paldo
+1 847 791-8085
alyssa.paldo@finnpartners.com
Cairn Financial Advisers LLP(Nominated Adviser and Broker)Sandy Jamieson, Jo Turner
+44 (0) 207 213 0880
Sisu Partners Oy(Certified Adviser on Nasdaq First North)Juha KarttunenJukka J¤rvel¤
+358 (0)40 555 4727+358 (0)50 553 8990
About bexmarilimabBexmarilimab is Faronâ€™s wholly owned, investigational immunotherapy designed to overcome resistance to existing treatments and optimize clinical outcomes, by targeting myeloid cell function and igniting the immune system. Bexmarilimab binds to Clever-1, an immunosuppressive receptor found on macrophages leading to tumor growth and metastases (i.e. helps cancer evade the immune system). By targeting the Clever-1 receptor on macrophages, bexmarilimab alters the tumor microenvironment, reprogramming macrophages from an immunosuppressive (M2) state to an immunostimulatory (M1) one, upregulating interferon production and priming the immune system to attack tumors and sensitizing cancer cells to standard of care.
About Faron Pharmaceuticals LtdFaron (AIM: FARN, First North: FARON) is a global, clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company, focused on tackling cancers via novel immunotherapies. Its mission is to bring the promise of immunotherapy to a broader population by uncovering novel ways to control and harness the power of the immune system. The companyâ€™s lead asset, bexmarilimab, is a novel macrophage-guiding immunotherapy being investigated in multiple oncology settings. Further information is available at
www.faron.com
.
Forward-Looking Statements
This press release contains certain forward-looking statements relating to the business of Faron Pharmaceuticals. In addition, even if the actual results or development of Faron Pharmaceuticals are consistent with the forward-looking statements contained in this press release, those results or developments of Faron Pharmaceuticals may not be sustained in the future. In some cases, you can identify forward-looking statements by words such as â€œcould,â€ â€œshould,â€ â€œmay,â€ â€œexpects,â€ â€œanticipates,â€ â€œbelieves,â€ â€œintends,â€ â€œestimates,â€ â€œaims,â€ â€œtargets,â€ or similar words. These forward-looking statements are based largely on the current expectations of Faron Pharmaceuticals as of the date of this press release and are subject to a number of known and unknown risks and uncertainties and other factors that may cause actual results, performance or achievements to be materially different from any future results, performance or achievement expressed or implied by these forward-looking statements. In particular, the expectations of Faron Pharmaceuticals could be affected by, among other things, uncertainties and delays involved in the development of product candidates, unexpected clinical trial results, unexpected regulatory actions or delays, competition in general, currency fluctuations, inflation, changes in tariff policies, political or macroeconomic developments, and the ability to obtain or maintain patent or other proprietary intellectual property protection. Success in preclinical studies or earlier clinical trials may not be indicative of results in future clinical trials. In light of these risks and uncertainties, there can be n

latest.title: Faron Pharmaceuticals Ltd: Director Dealing
latest.content:
Today 08:00
Faron Pharmaceuticals Ltd | Company announcement | March 25, 2026 at 10:00:00 EET
TURKU, FINLAND â€" Faron Pharmaceuticals Ltd. (AIM: FARN, First North: FARON), a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company focused on developing novel immunotherapies, announces today that Sirpa Jalkanen, Scientific founder of the Company and spouse of Board member Markku Jalkanen, subscribed 100,008 ordinary shares in Faron at price of â‚¬0.5 per share on 24 March 2026, pursuant to the Rights Issue announced by the Company on 10 March 2026.
Further details are set out in the Notification of Dealing Form below.
For more information, please contact:
IR Partners, Finland(Media)Kare Laukkanen
+358 50 553 9535 / +44 7 469 766 223
kare.laukkanen@irpartners.fi
FINN Partners, US(Media) Alyssa Paldo
+1 847 791-8085
alyssa.paldo@finnpartners.com
Cairn Financial Advisers LLP(Nominated Adviser and Broker)Sandy Jamieson, Jo Turner
+44 (0) 207 213 0880
Sisu Partners Oy(Certified Adviser on Nasdaq First North)Juha KarttunenJukka J¤rvel¤
+358 (0)40 555 4727+358 (0)50 553 8990
About bexmarilimabBexmarilimab is Faronâ€™s wholly owned, investigational immunotherapy designed to overcome resistance to existing treatments and optimize clinical outcomes, by targeting myeloid cell function and igniting the immune system. Bexmarilimab binds to Clever-1, an immunosuppressive receptor found on macrophages leading to tumor growth and metastases (i.e. helps cancer evade the immune system). By targeting the Clever-1 receptor on macrophages, bexmarilimab alters the tumor microenvironment, reprogramming macrophages from an immunosuppressive (M2) state to an immunostimulatory (M1) one, upregulating interferon production and priming the immune system to attack tumors and sensitizing cancer cells to standard of care.
About Faron Pharmaceuticals LtdFaron (AIM: FARN, First North: FARON) is a global, clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company, focused on tackling cancers via novel immunotherapies. Its mission is to bring the promise of immunotherapy to a broader population by uncovering novel ways to control and harness the power of the immune system. The Companyâ€™s lead asset is bexmarilimab, a novel anti-Clever-1 humanized antibody, with the potential to remove immunosuppression of cancers through reprogramming myeloid cell function. Bexmarilimab is being investigated in Phase I/II clinical trials as a potential therapy for patients with hematological cancers in combination with other standard treatments. Further information is available at
www.faron.com

(AIM: FARN, First North: FARON), a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company focused on developing novel immunotherapies, announces today that Pacta sunt servanda Oy, an entity wholly owned by Vesa Karvonen, General Counsel of the Company, subscribed 72,000 ordinary shares in Faron at price of EUR 0.5 per share on 18 March 2026, pursuant to the rights issue announced by the Company on 10 March 2026.

By targeting the Clever-1 receptor on macrophages, bexmarilimab alters the tumor microenvironment, reprogramming macrophages from an immunosuppressive (M2) state to an immunostimulatory (M1) one, upregulating interferon production and priming the immune system to attack tumors and sensitizing cancer cells to standard of care.
About Faron Pharmaceuticals LtdFaron (AIM: FARN, First North: FARON) is a global, clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company, focused on tackling cancers via novel immunotherapies. Its mission is to bring the promise of immunotherapy to a broader population by uncovering novel ways to control and harness the power of the immune system. The Companyâ€™s lead asset is bexmarilimab, a novel anti-Clever-1 humanized antibody, with the potential to remove immunosuppression of cancers through reprogramming myeloid cell function. Bexmarilimab is being investigated in Phase I/II clinical trials as a potential therapy for patients with hematological cancers in combination with other standard treatments. company_30s.what_they_do: Faron Pharmaceuticals develops antibody‑based medicines for autoimmune and inflammatory disorders, mainly targeting conditions such as rheumatoid arthritis and other chronic inflammatory diseases.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The biologics market is expanding rapidly, and Faron’s lead asset, bexmarilimab, could offer a novel treatment option where current therapies are limited or costly. Their focus on a niche but high‑need area gives them a defensible position within the broader pharma landscape.
company_30s.current_state: The firm is currently raising capital through a rights offering to strengthen its balance sheet and fund the development of bexmarilimab. The share price has slid sharply in recent months, but the rights issue provides a potential floor and a chance for institutional buyers to step in.

rns_narrative: Faron is executing a €40 million rights offering at €0.435 per share, approved by the Finnish regulator, to shore up cash and keep the pipeline moving.
The company has updated its financial calendar and AGM dates, signalling a move toward greater transparency and governance.
Recent director‑dealing activity and the announcement of the rights issue suggest management is actively managing capital to maintain liquidity.

crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bulls point to the rights price being below the current market level, implying a cheap entry for institutional investors and a potential catalyst for a bounce.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears caution that the rights issue may dilute existing shareholders and that the pipeline still faces regulatory and clinical hurdles.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The community was largely silent until the rights announcement, after which a small wave of optimism appeared, but overall sentiment remains cautious.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat – no recent search activity, so any news could trigger a surprise move.

near_term_catalysts: Closing of the rights offering – the company will need to see shareholder uptake to confirm the floor.
AGM decisions on the use of the raised funds and any new partnership announcements.
Any clinical milestone or positive data release for bexmarilimab that could validate the pipeline.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a volume spike that exceeds the 20‑day average by at least 30 % and a price that trades consistently above the rights price of €0.435. Positive press on the pipeline or a partnership announcement would be a strong confirmation.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early stage of a recovery cycle; the APEX score indicates tight compression, so a bounce could start soon after the rights issue is settled.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the share price climbs to roughly €0.75 (about 50 % above the current level), the upside is around 50 %. With a typical green‑color signal (≈40 % win rate), a few successful bounces can offset many small losses, creating a favorable risk/reward asymmetry.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: If the rights issue fails to attract buyers or the pipeline stalls, the share could stay near the current level or even fall further. The worst realistic scenario is a 100 % loss of the current price if the company runs out of cash or faces a regulatory setback.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: ~€0.25 per share (≈50 %). Potential downside: close to 0 % from current price, but if the rights issue fails, the downside could be the full €0.50. The asymmetry favors a small loss for a large gain.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter after the rights issue closes if the price holds above €0.44 and volume remains above the 20‑day average. Size the position for 10 % of your discretionary capital and place a stop 10 % below the entry to protect against a brief retracement.
playbook.if_watching: Wait for a volume spike of at least 30 % over the 20‑day average and a sustained price above €0.44. Positive pipeline news or a partnership announcement would be the trigger.
playbook.if_passing: None identified – no imminent delisting, bankruptcy, or fraud allegations.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Faron’s deep crash has created a cheap entry point, the rights issue offers a clear catalyst, and the upside potential is asymmetric, making it a contrarian bullish play if the market accepts the new capital structure.
